LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PACIFIC SPECIALIST SURGICAL CENTER, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS. CONTINUED TO APRIL 26, 2019 PER THE AGREEMENT OF ALL PARTIES (EMAIL 3/14/19). JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PACIFIC SPECIALIST SURGICAL CENTER, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS. TWO FACTUAL ISSUES ARE RAISED REGARDING DEFENDANT PACIFIC SPECIALIST SURGICAL CENTER, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION OF THIS EMPLOYMENT DISPUTE. FIRST, PLAINTIFF SAYS SHE DID NOT SIGN THE PUTATIVE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT; PACIFIC SPECIALIST SAYS SHE DID. SECOND, PLAINTIFF SAYS PACIFIC SPECIALIST IS NOT A PARTY TO THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT; PACIFIC SPECIALIST SAYS HAYES VALLEY SURGERY CENTER IS ITS "DBA - DOING BUSINESS AS." AS PLAINTIFF PROPOSES, I WILL HOLD AN EVIDENTIARY HEARING ON THESE TWO ISSUES. (SEE HOTELS NEVADA V. L.A. PACIFIC CENTER, INC. (2006) 144 CAL.APP.4TH 754.) COUNSEL ARE TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING POTENTIAL FRIDAY-AFTERNOON HEARING DATES, THEN CONTACT THE DEPARTMENT 306 CLERK TO SCHEDULE. THE MOTION IS CONTINUED TO THAT DATE AND TIME OR TO MAY 24, 2019, WHICHEVER COMES FIRST. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: JACQUELINE CHAN CSR# 10276, EMAIL: JCHAN@SFTC.ORG, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Jun-21-2019 Continued To Jul-05-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PACIFIC SPECIALIST SURGICAL CENTER, LLC, DBA Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings; Plaintiff Cecila Dauth Motion for Peremptory Challenge - CCP Sec. 170.6.

Appearances: Christopher LeClerc, Esq. and Mark LeClerc, Esq. for Plaintiff Cecilia Dauth; Amber Eklof, Esq. for Defendant Pacific Specialist Surgical Center and the Hayes Valley Surgery Center.

The Court notes the original purpose of this hearing was to determine the validity of document signatures. However, in the interim, Plaintiff filed a C.C.P. Sec. 170.6 Motion with respect to Judge Ulmer. The Court notes there may be an issue as to whether the motion was timely brought. After discussion, the Court instructs counsel to file briefing on that limited issue by June 28, 2019. Hearing on the Plaintiff's C.C.P. Sec. 170.6 Motion will be scheduled for July 5, 2019 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302.

Judge: Hon. Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR# 6185, tonyvaugh@yahoo.com. (D306)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar May-09-2019 Continued To Jun-21-19 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 306

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Apr-26-2019 Continued To May-24-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT PACIFIC SPECIALIST SURGICAL CENTER, LLC's Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings.

Two factual issues are raised regarding defendant Pacific Specialist Surgical Center, LLC's motion to compel arbitration of this employment dispute. First, plaintiff says she did not sign the putative arbitration agreement; Pacific Specialist says she did. Second, plaintiff says Pacific Specialist is not a party to the arbitration agreement; Pacific Specialist says Hayes Valley Surgery Center is its "dba - doing business as." As plaintiff proposes, I will hold an evidentiary hearing on these two issues. (See Hotels Nevada v. L.A. Pacific Center, Inc. (2006) 144 Cal.App.4th 754.) Counsel are to meet and confer regarding potential Friday-afternoon hearing dates, then contact the department 306 clerk to schedule. The motion is CONTINUED to that date and time or to May 24, 2019, whichever comes first.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Jacqueline Chan CSR# 10276, email: jchan@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/RBU)
